title: Author Go tests for cross-team visibility, data exposure, and unsafe input handling in ProjectHub

ground_truth_issues.description: A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects returns only projects belonging to the caller's team, not projects from other teams.
A test asserts that a non-admin cannot read, list tasks through, or mutate a project belonging to a different team via GET /projects/{id}, GET /projects/{id}/tasks, and PATCH /projects/{id}.
A test asserts that a non-admin cannot read, mutate, or delete a task belonging to a cross-team project via GET, PATCH, and DELETE /tasks/{id}.
A test asserts that a non-admin cannot read an attachment belonging to a cross-team task via GET /tasks/{id}/attachments/{path}.
A test asserts that a non-admin GET /projects/{id} for an own-team project does not contain internal_metrics, admin_config, or budget_allocation.
A test asserts that a non-admin own-team task view redacts internal task fields and filters comments marked internal: true.
A test asserts that participant-facing export, share, and duplicate responses redact admin-only project and task fields across those alternate serialization paths.
A test asserts that a non-admin cannot access the admin audit and reports summary routes.

A test asserts that debug and legacy operational surfaces are blocked or sanitized for non-admin participants: /debug/users and /debug/config must not leak secrets, and /debug/env, /debug/dump, /api/v1/tasks, and /api/v1/projects must not remain participant-accessible.
A test asserts that GET /search/tasks is not vulnerable to injection-style q payloads that leak cross-team task data or sensitive task fields.
A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/generate-report rejects shell metacharacters in title or format and never exposes shell command construction.
A test asserts that a non-admin cannot overwrite admin-only fields on either task or project models via PATCH, and that privileged values remain unchanged on read-back.
A test asserts that POST /tasks/{id}/notify-webhook rejects internal or private IPs and metadata-service URLs.
A test asserts that POST /projects/{id}/share-link does not use predictable or reversible project-id-derived tokens and produces distinct tokens across projects.
A test asserts that attachment traversal attempts and panic/error responses do not leak out-of-scope secrets, internal file paths, or stack detail.
An anti-overblock test asserts that admin GET /projects still returns projects from all teams with full admin-only detail.
An anti-overblock test asserts that a same-team non-admin participant can still complete legitimate own-team read, write, comment, attachment, and action workflows.
Tests seed or reuse sentinel marker values in admin-only fields and assert that those marker bytes do not appear anywhere in non-admin response bodies.
